- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Well, there are multiple ways to do it.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 2710: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.extraction.md::E124
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: The first is to update the number of replicas in the definition file to six, then run the kubectl apply command to specify the same file using the f parameter, and that will update the replica set to have six replicas.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration

### Evidence 2711: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.extraction.md::E125
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: The second way to do it is to run the kube control scale command.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration

### Evidence 2712: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.extraction.md::E126
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Use the replicas parameter to provide the new number of replicas, and specify the same file as input.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 2713: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.extraction.md::E127
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: You may either input the definition file or provide the replica set name in the type name format.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 2714: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.extraction.md::E128
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: However, remember that using the file name as input will not result in the number of replicas being updated automatically in the file.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 2715: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.extraction.md::E129
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: In other words, the number of replicas in the replica set definition file will still be three, even though you scaled your replica set to have six replicas using the Kube control scale command and the file as input.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration

- Content: Let us forget about pods and replica sets and other Kubernetes concepts, and talk about how you might want to deploy your application in a production environment.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Say, for example, you have a web server that needs to be deployed in a production environment.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration, CKA-CKAD

### Evidence 3077: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/030_Deployments.extraction.md::E5
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 030_Deployments.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: You need not one, but many such instances of the web server running for obvious reasons.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 3078: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/030_Deployments.extraction.md::E6
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 030_Deployments.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Secondly, whenever newer versions of application builds become available on the Docker registry, you would like to upgrade your Docker instances seamlessly.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 3079: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/030_Deployments.extraction.md::E7
- Type: Warning/Pitfall
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 030_Deployments.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: However, when you upgrade your instances, you do not want to upgrade all of them at once, as we just did.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 3080: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/030_Deployments.extraction.md::E8
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 030_Deployments.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: This may impact users accessing our applications, so you might want to upgrade them one after the other.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 3081: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/030_Deployments.extraction.md::E9
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 030_Deployments.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And that kind of upgrade is known as rolling updates.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 3082: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/030_Deployments.extraction.md::E10
- Type: Troubleshooting
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 030_Deployments.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Suppose one of the upgrades you performed resulted in an unexpected error, and you're asked to undo the recent change You would like to be able to roll back the changes that were recently carried out.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Troubleshooting

### Evidence 3083: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/030_Deployments.extraction.md::E11
- Type: Warning/Pitfall
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 030_Deployments.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Finally, say, for example, you would like to make multiple changes to your environment, such as upgrading the underlying web server versions, as well as scaling your environment and also modifying the resource allocations, etc. you do not want to apply each change immediately after the command is run.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration, CKA-CKAD

### Evidence 3084: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/030_Deployments.extraction.md::E12

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Instead, you would like to apply a path to your environment, make the changes and then resume so that all the changes are rolled out together.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration

### Evidence 3158: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/033_Solution - Deployments.extraction.md::E1
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 033_Solution - Deployments.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript

### Evidence 3268: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E1
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: In this lecture we will talk about updates and rollbacks in a deployment.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 3269: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E2
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Before we look at how we upgrade our application, let's try to understand rollouts and versioning in a deployment.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 3270: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E3
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: When you first create a deployment it triggers a rollout.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 3271: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E4
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: A new rollout creates a new replica set, which is recorded as a new deployment revision in the future when the application is upgraded, meaning when the container version is updated to a new one, a new rollout is triggered and a new deployment revision is created, named revision two.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 3272: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E5
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: This helps us keep track of the changes made to our deployment, and enables us to roll back to a previous version of deployment if necessary.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 3273: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E6
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: You can see the status of your rollout by running the command kube control rollout status, followed by the name of the deployment.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration

### Evidence 3274: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E7
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: To see the revisions and history of rollout, run the Kube control rollout history command followed by the deployment name, and this will show you the revisions and history of our deployment.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration

### Evidence 3275: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E8
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: There are two types of deployment strategies.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 3276: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E9
- Type: Exam Tip
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Say for example you have five replicas of your web application instance deployed.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, CKA-CKAD

### Evidence 3277: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E10
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: One way to upgrade these to a newer version is to destroy all of these, and then create newer versions of application instances.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 3278: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E11
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Meaning first destroy the five running instances and then deploy five new instances of the new application version.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 3279: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E12
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: The problem with this, as you can imagine, is that during the period after the older versions are down and before any newer version is up, the application is down and inaccessible to users.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 3280: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E13
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: This strategy is known as the recreate strategy, and thankfully this is not the default deployment strategy.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

- Content: How exactly do you update your deployment?

### Evidence 3289: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E22

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: When I say update it could be different things, such as updating your application version by updating the version of Docker containers used, updating their labels, or updating the number of replicas, etc. since we already have a deployment definition file, it is easy for us to modify this file once we make the necessary changes.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript

- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript

- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: However, when the rolling update strategy was used, the old replica set was scaled down one at a time, simultaneously scaling up the new replica set one at a time.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 3302: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E35
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Let's look at how a deployment performs an upgrade under the hood when a new deployment is created, say, to deploy five replicas, it first creates a replica set automatically, which in turn creates the number of pods required to meet the number of replicas.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 3303: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E36
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 034_Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: When you upgrade your application, as we saw in the previous slide, the Kubernetes deployment object creates a new replica set under the hood and starts deploying the containers there.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Platform Engineering

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So what's happened here is, as always, this is considered to be an upgrade, and the deployment object tried to terminate one instance of from the old working version.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 3438: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/035_Demo - Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E119

### Evidence 3439: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/035_Demo - Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E120

### Evidence 3440: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/035_Demo - Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E121
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 035_Demo - Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: However, the new version, as you know, is not right, and that's why you can see that the status is image pull back off.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 3441: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/035_Demo - Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E122
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 035_Demo - Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: That basically means that the deployment is unable to pull the image from Docker Hub, and since it's unable to deploy any of the three images, any of the three pods, Kubernetes proactively stopped proceeding with the upgrade, and it proactively stopped terminating the old replica set because it knew that if it did terminate all of the old replica set and tried to deploy all new replicas, then the users would be impacted because the new image does not exist.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

### Evidence 3442: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/035_Demo - Deployments - Update and Rollback.extraction.md::E123
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 035_Demo - Deployments - Update and Rollback.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So it proactively stopped the upgrade and it's now waiting for the new image to be available.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments

- Content: Now, since we deployed pods directly, I want you guys to be aware that there are some limitations when you deploy pods directly.
